features.summary: Apple reported Q4 FY2024 revenue of $94.9 billion, up 6% year-over-year and a September quarter record. iPhone revenue reached $46.2 billion (up 6%) with growth across all geographic segments, while Services hit an all-time record of $25 billion (up 12%). The company launched Apple Intelligence features in US English on iOS 18.1, with adoption rates twice as fast as the previous year's comparable release. Mac revenue grew 2% to $7.7 billion and iPad revenue increased 8% to $7 billion, while Wearables declined 3% to $9 billion.
features.management_outlook: Apple expects December quarter total company revenue to grow low- to mid-single digits year-over-year, with Services continuing double-digit growth at rates similar to fiscal 2024. The company anticipates gross margins between 46-47% and operating expenses of $15.3-15.5 billion. Management emphasized the staged rollout of Apple Intelligence features, with additional capabilities launching in December including ChatGPT integration and localized English support for several countries, followed by more languages starting in April 2025.
features.risks: The company faces potential supply constraints on iPhone 16 Pro models through October, though management expects to resolve these soon. Apple Intelligence rollout is geographically staged rather than simultaneous, which could impact normal demand patterns across regions. The guidance assumes macroeconomic conditions don't worsen from current projections. There are ongoing legal uncertainties regarding the DOJ case related to Google revenue sharing agreements.
features.key_drivers: Apple Intelligence adoption is showing strong early momentum with 18.1 iOS adoption twice as fast as the previous year, positioning it as a compelling upgrade driver. The installed base reached all-time highs across all product categories and geographic segments, with high customer satisfaction rates (95-98% across products). Services growth is supported by over 1 billion paid subscriptions growing double-digits, with the recurring portion growing faster than transactional services. International markets showed strength, particularly India setting all-time revenue records and China showing sequential improvement.
